content.cn_text: 导师:。沙伦和小李，你们在上周的演讲中谈到了数字鸿沟——那些能有效使用互联网等通信工具的人和不能有效使用互联网的人之间的差距。你比较了北爱尔兰和中国东南部的情况。对，我让你做一些销售评估，看一下你的演讲视频，思考一下评估你的三个主要标准——内容、结构和技术。当你看这个演讲的时候，你认为它最大的特点是什么?沙龙吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: So. Sharon and Xiao Li, in your presentation last week you were talking about the digital divide - the gap between those who can effectively use communication tools such as the Internet, and those who can't. And you compared the situation here in Northern Ireland with South-East China. Right, so I asked you to do some sell evaluation, watching the video of your presentation and thinking about the three main criteria you're assessed by - content, structure and technique. What do you think was the strongest feature of the presentation, when you watched it? Sharon?

content.cn_text: 雪伦:好。实际上我很惊讶，因为我很紧张，但是当我看视频的时候，它并没有显示出我预期的那么多。
content.en_text: Sharon: Well. I was surprised actually, because l felt quite nervous but when I watched the video, it didn't show as much as I expected.

content.cn_text: 导师:那你要符合哪个标准呢?
content.en_text: Tutor: So which of the criteria would that come under?

content.cn_text: 莎伦:嗯，信心?
content.en_text: Sharon: Er, confidence?

content.cn_text: 导师:小李，这不是标准之一吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: That's not actually one of the criteria as such Xiao Li?

content.cn_text: 小李:技术?这是肢体语言和眼神交流，不是吗?嗯，我不认为我看起来那么自信，但我认为，我们的技术总体上是好的，就像我们设计和使用ppt幻灯片的方式一样。
content.en_text: Xiao Li: Technique? It's body language and eye contact, isn't it. Well, I didn't think I looked all that confident, but I think, that our technique was generally good like the way we designed and used the PowerPoint slides.

content.cn_text: 导师:嗯。所以你们俩都对演示的那一面感到最开心吗?好的，在消极方面，如果你能再来一次，你会改变什么?
content.en_text: Tutor: Mmm. So you both feel happiest about that side of the presentation? OK, now on the negative side, what would you change if you could do it again?

content.cn_text: 小李:嗯，一开始我以为介绍会是一个问题，不过实际上我认为还可以。我们定义了我们的条款并确定了关键问题。它更接近尾声，结论不是太糟糕，但问题是问题，我们真的没有预料到会有任何问题，所以我们没有想那么多。
content.en_text: Xiao Li: Well, at first I'd thought that the introduction was going to be the problem but actually I think that was OK. We defined our terms and identified key issues. It was more towards the end, the conclusion wasn't too bad but the problem was the questions,we hadn't really expected there'd be any so we hadn't thought about them that much.

content.cn_text: 导师:嗯，好的。还有别的事吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Uhuh OK. Anything else?

content.cn_text: 莎伦:嗯，就像小李说的，我觉得结论还可以，但是当我看视频的时候，我觉得解决方案的部分似乎很弱。
content.en_text: Sharon: Well, like Xiao Li says, I thought the conclusion was OK, but when I watched us on the video I thought the section on solutions seemed rather weak.

content.cn_text: 导师:嗯。你知道为什么吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Mmm. Can you think why?

content.cn_text: 莎伦:嗯，我们解释了中国和北爱尔兰的人们是如何应对数字鸿沟的，但我想我们并没有真正评估任何项目或想法，只是列出了一个清单。这也是人们最后问我们的问题。
content.en_text: Sharon: Well, we explained what people are doing about the digital divide in China and Northern Ireland but I suppose we didn't really evaluate any of the projects or ideas, it was just a list. And that was what people were asking us about at the end mostly.

content.cn_text: 老师:好的。现在，我还要求你们从其他学生那里得到一些同行评价。
content.en_text: Tutor: OK. Now, I also asked you to get some peer evaluation, from the other students.

content.cn_text: 莎伦:是的，嗯，人们说这很有趣，比如在中国，人们比在北爱尔兰更常使用互联网购物。他们说有时候听不懂，因为我们说得太快了。但是当我们看视频的时候，我们并不这么认为。
content.en_text: Sharon: Yes, er, well, people said it was interesting, like the fact that in China the Internet was used more for shopping than in Northern Ireland. They said sometimes it was a bit hard to understand because we were talking quite fast .. but we didn't think so when we watched the video.

content.cn_text: 导师:不，这有点不同，因为你已经知道所有这些信息了。如果你是第一次听到，你需要更多的时间来消化。这就是为什么说明演讲的结构和组织是很重要的。
content.en_text: Tutor: No, it's a bit different though, because you know all this information already.Mmm. If you're hearing it for the first time, you need more time to process it ... that's why signposting the structure and organisation of the talk is important.

content.cn_text: 小李:看起来还行，没人说这是个问题。有些人说我们可以在幻灯片上放更多……就像其他一些小组几乎把他们所说的一切都写在视觉上一样，……幻灯片很好，他们有……要点
content.en_text: Xiao Li: That seemed OK, no one mentioned that as a problem. Some people said that we could have had more on the slides.. like some ot the other groups had nearly everything they said written up on the visuals as well, ... the slides were good, they had... me key points

content.cn_text: 老师:是的。
content.en_text: Tutor: Yes.

content.cn_text: 莎伦:大多数人说我们有很好的眼神交流和肢体语言。他们都指出我们会超支。他们都说我们多了五分钟，但我们事后在录像上计时，结果只有三分钟。
content.en_text: Sharon: And most people said we had quite good eye contact and body language. They all pointed out we'd overrun .. they all said we were five minutes over but we timed it afterwards on the video and it was only three minutes.

content.cn_text: 小李:一开始我们对背景阅读有点不确定，但我认为我们在那段时间里尽了最大努力。不管怎么说，没有人在内容下评论，但结果是他们喜欢我们对北爱尔兰和中国都做了研究，而大多数人只是基于一个国家的研究。我们设法从互联网上获得了相当多的数据，尽管我们必须自己做分析，我们也在两个国家做了自己的调查。所以这门课给了我们最好的内容反馈，但这一切都还可以。
content.en_text: Xiao Li: We were a bit unsure about the background reading at first, but I think we did as much as we could in the time. Anyway, no one commented on that under content, but one thing that diet come out was that they liked the fact we'd done research on both Northern Ireland and China, most other people had just based their research on one country. We managed to get quite a lot of data from the Internet, although we had to do our own analysis and we did our own surveys as well in both countries. So the class gave us best feedback for content but it was all OK.

content.cn_text: 老师:对。嗯，这和我给你的反馈很相似。你所做的大量工作和你的研究方法给我留下了深刻的印象。所以我给你们的内容打满分，5分。演讲的结构很好，但不如内容好，所以我给了四个，技巧也一样。那么，克隆。
content.en_text: Tutor: Right. Well, that's quite similar to the feedback I'm giving you. I was very impressed by the amount of work you'd done and by your research methodology .. So actually I'm giving you full marks for content, five. The structure of the presentation was good, but not quite as good as the content, so I gave that four, and the same for technique. So, well clone.

content.cn_text: 小李/莎伦:谢谢
content.en_text: Xiao Li/Sharon: Thank you

content.cn_text: 导师:现在，下一个阶段是写你的报告，这里给你一些提示。首先，在你的演讲中，你觉得你的结尾很突然——你突然就不说话了。这不是一个大问题，但是想想你报告的结束语——你想把它圆满。我之前忘记提到的一件事是，我觉得非常重要的一点是，在您给出结果之后，您解释了的局限性。
content.en_text: Tutor: Now, the next stage is to write up your report So just a few pointers for you here.First of all, in your presentation think your ending was rather abrupt - you suddenly just stopped talking. It wasn't a big problem but think about your closing sentences in your report - you want to round it off well. One thing I forgot to mention earlier was that I felt a very strong point was that after you'd given your results, you explained them  limitations.

content.cn_text: 小李:我们在中国没有一个非常可靠的年龄样本?
content.en_text: Xiao Li: The fact that we didn't have a very reliable sample in terms of age in China?

content.cn_text: 老师:是的。这部分。所以别忘了把它写进去。你有一些很好的图表，但也许你可以充实一下文献综述。如果你想的话，我可以稍后给你一些想法。好的，你还有什么问题要问的吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Yes. that section. So don't forget to include that. And you had some excellent charts and diagrams, but maybe you could flesh out the literature review a bit. I can give you some ideas for that later on if you want. OK, is there anything else you want to ask?

groups.desc:
Questions 21-23
Answer the questions below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

items.title: What do Sharon and Xiao Li agree was the strongest aspect of their presentation?
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是"technique"（技巧），因为在对话中，Sharon和Xiao Li最终一致认为他们的演讲技巧是最强的方面。首先，导师问他们认为演讲中最强的方面是什么。虽然Sharon开始提到了自信，但导师指出这不是评估标准之一。然后Xiao Li提到了技巧，并具体解释了包括身体语言、眼神交流和PowerPoint的使用。更重要的是，导师随后问道"So you both feel happiest about that side of the presentation?"（所以你们俩对演讲的这方面最满意？），这个问题中的"happiest about"就是题目中"strongest aspect"的同义替换，进一步确认了"technique"是他们认为最强的方面。因此，根据对话内容和上下文，我们可以确定"technique"是Sharon和Xiao Li一致认为他们演讲最强的方面。

items.title: Which part of their presentation was Xiao Li least happy with?
items.answer: answering the questions / answering students' questions / the questions / students' questions
items.analysis: 在这道题中，我们需要找出Xiao Li最不满意的演讲部分。通过分析原文，我们可以看到Xiao Li提到了几个部分：介绍、结论和回答问题。他用"wasn't too bad"来描述结论，这意味着结论还可以。但是当谈到回答问题时，他明确使用了"problem"这个词，并解释说他们没有预料到会有问题，也没有充分准备。这明确表明回答问题是他最不满意的部分。所以，正确答案是"(answering) (the/students') questions"。记住，在听力考试中，我们要特别注意负面词汇（如"problem"）和表示对比的表达，这些通常能帮助我们找到关键信息。

items.title: Which section does Sharon feel they should have discussed in more depth?
items.analysis: 在这道题中，我们需要找出Sharon认为应该更深入讨论的部分。从原文中，我们可以看到Sharon提到了两个部分：结论（conclusion）和解决方案（solutions）。她说结论部分"还可以"（OK），但解决方案部分"看起来相当薄弱"（seemed rather weak）。当我们看到"weak"这个词时，就应该意识到这可能是需要改进或更深入讨论的部分。因此，Sharon认为应该更深入讨论的是解决方案（solutions）部分。这正是题目所问的内容。记住，在听力中，像"weak"、"insufficient"、"inadequate"这样的词往往暗示着需要改进或更深入讨论的地方。

groups.desc:
Questions 24-27
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Sharon and Xiao Li were surprised when the class said

options.title: C
options.content: their talk was not well organised.

items.analysis: 正确答案是A，因为原文中明确提到班级同学反馈说Sharon和Xiao Li说话太快，有时候难以理解。这正是题目中所问的"Sharon和Xiao Li感到惊讶的内容"。他们在看自己的视频时并没有觉得说得太快，所以对同学们的这个反馈感到惊讶。

items.title: The class gave Sharon and Xiao Li conflicting feedback on their
items.analysis: 文中提到了两种关于视觉材料使用的矛盾反馈：一方面说幻灯片可以包含更多内容，另一方面又说幻灯片很好，包含了关键点。这种矛盾的反馈正好符合题目要求的"conflicting feedback"（矛盾的反馈）。其他方面，如时间和眼神交流，都没有出现矛盾的反馈，因此B选项（use of visuals）是正确答案。

items.title: The class thought that the presentation was different from the others because

options.title: B
options.content: the data collection was more wide-ranging.

options.title: C
options.content: the background reading was more extensive

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为原文明确指出他们的研究涵盖了北爱尔兰和中国两个国家，而其他人只基于一个国家进行研究。这表明他们的数据收集范围更广。此外，文中还提到他们从互联网获取了大量数据，并在两个国家进行了自己的调查，进一步证实了他们数据收集的广泛性。

items.title: Which bar chart represents the marks given by the tutor?
items.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为导师给出的分数是：内容（content）5分，结构（structure）4分，技巧（technique）4分。这与图表C的表示完全一致。导师明确地给出了三个方面的分数，并且只有图表C准确反映了这些分数。

groups.desc:
Questions 28-30
Complete the sentences below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect:
The tutor says that the $15757$ of the presentation seemed rather sudden.
The tutor praises the students' discussion of the $15758$ of their results.
The tutor suggests that they could extend the $15759$ review in their report.

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解原文中对演讲结尾的描述。导师说"你们的结尾（ending）相当突兀（abrupt）"，并进一步解释为"你们突然就停止了讲话"。这里的"ending"就是我们需要填入的答案。题干中使用了"sudden"这个词，原文中用"abrupt"和"suddenly"来表达相同的意思，这是一个很好的信号词。而"presentation"作为定位词，帮助我们找到了讨论演讲的相关段落。记住，在填空题中，我们需要填入原文中出现的确切单词，而不是同义词。因此，尽管"conclusion"或"closing"在意义上可能相似，但正确答案仍然是原文中使用的"ending"。这道题考察了你对细节的把握和对同义表达的理解能力。

这道题的关键在于理解老师赞扬的具体内容。文中老师明确表示，学生在给出研究结果后解释了这些结果的"limitations"（局限性）是一个"very strong point"（非常强的优点）。这正是题目所问的：老师赞扬学生对结果的哪方面进行了讨论。"limitations"这个词直接对应了题目中的空白处，因为它是老师特别指出并赞扬的内容。在学术研究中，认识并讨论研究的局限性是非常重要的，这显示了研究者的批判性思维和对研究的深入理解。所以，老师特别表扬了学生这一点，这也是为什么"limitations"是正确答案。

这道题的答案是"literature"，因为题干中问到"The tutor suggests that they could extend the [   ] review in their report"。在原文中，导师明确建议学生可以"flesh out the literature review"，即充实文献综述。这里的"flesh out"是一个重要的信号词，表示"扩展"或"充实"的意思，与题干中的"extend"相对应。而"literature review"正是学术报告中的一个重要组成部分，指的是对相关研究文献的回顾和总结。因此，"literature"是填入空白处的正确答案，既符合原文意思，又符合学术写作的术语用法。注意，虽然原文中也提到了"charts"和"diagrams"，但这些并不是需要扩展的部分，相反，导师认为这些已经做得很好了。所以，理解上下文和准确识别信号词对于回答这个问题至关重要。
